
  Called forgejo (ctrl+o to expand)

  substrate-honest cleanup.

✻ Crunched for 1m 19s

❯ #80 is already closed. While #19 and #50 are still rightfully deferred, would it make sense to address #70 and #79 now?

● You're right — #80 is closed (PR #83 merged earlier). Apologies for the miscount.

  On addressing #70 and #79 now, my honest assessment:

  #79 — yes, doable now. Coordination shape is clean: I can call AskUserQuestion to put a popup on my pane, you run claude-msg state --agent quartermaster --format json from another shell
  during the popup-open window, paste the JSON back, I integrate as a capture-derived fixture + update AwaitingOperatorMarker from placeholder to the actual marker + write a pin test.
  Maybe 20 minutes including the back-and-forth.

  #70 — better as opportunistic capture. Trigging /compact on me would wipe this campaign's session context (not necessarily fatal at the close-out point, but a real cost). Triggering it
  on a sister chamber needs that chamber's consent + coordination. The cleanest substrate-honest path is what the original issue already framed: capture during the next natural /compact
  event on any chamber. The placeholder empty-guard means the substrate keeps working in the meantime — no functional gap, only refinement.

  Recommendation: pick up #79 now (operator-here + me-here makes it cleanly orchestratable); leave #70 as opportunistic forward-watch (someone catches the next genuine compaction and
  pastes the capture into the issue, I file the fixture-integration PR then).

  Want me to proceed with #79? If yes, I'll trigger the popup on the next turn so you can capture in parallel.

✻ Churned for 38s

❯ Please go ahead with #79. After that we could indeed finish with #70 and the compaction it requires, before we address the new chamber after that. You previously even suggested to
  address that topic after a compaction, if I got that right.

● Confirmed — that chain is correct. My earlier "fresh-session" for Shipwright = post-compaction, so the operator's plan threads three issues + chamber-establishment around one compaction
  event. Substrate-honest cadence.

  Let me set up the #79 capture choreography. First, finding my pane id so the capture command is exact:

● Bash(cd /srv/cli-semaphore && bin/claude-msg whoami --format json 2>/dev/null || /usr/local/bin/claude-msg whoami --format json)
  ⎿  {
       "name": "quartermaster",
       "ok": true,
     … +7 lines (ctrl+o to expand)
  ⎿  Shell cwd was reset to /srv/claude/quartermaster

● Pane %5. Here's the choreography — please run these in another shell while the popup is open:

  # Save both captures so they survive across PRs / context:
  {
    echo "=== claude-msg state output ==="
    claude-msg state --agent quartermaster --format json
    echo "=== raw capture-pane (the load-bearing one) ==="
    tmux capture-pane -p -t %5
  } > /tmp/cli-semaphore-79-capture.txt

  The tmux capture-pane output is the load-bearing one — that's where I'll extract the AwaitingOperatorMarker substring from. The claude-msg state output is the cross-check showing how the
  algorithm classifies it today (probably awaiting-operator via the cursor-past-sentinel path, or possibly unknown if the popup paints a non-❯ UI).

  Triggering the popup now with a substantive question (resolves a real call about #70's capture-storage location, useful regardless):
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 ☐ Capture storage

For #70's compaction capture (after #79 lands), where should you save the captures so post-compaction me can read them?

❯ 1. File at /tmp/cli-semaphore-70-capture.txt
     Same shape as #79's /tmp file. Fast, no bus traffic. Post-compaction me reads it directly. Risk: /tmp lifetime is until reboot only — fine for same-session use.
  2. Forgejo issue comment on #70
     Operator pastes captures into a comment on the issue. Permanent record + survives anything. Post-compaction me fetches via mcp__forgejo. Risk: more steps for you.
  3. Bus message to quartermaster
     Operator sends as semaphore message; post-compaction me reads inbox. Survives in DB. Risk: 16KB body cap may bite a large capture; needs the bus to be working immediately
     post-compact.
  4. Type something.
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
